channel.title: Taylor Series on Amit Rajan
channel.description: Recent content in Taylor Series on Amit Rajan

item.title: Measurement and Propagation of Error (Part 2)
item.description: Linear Combinations of Dependent Measurements : In the case of dependent measurement, to quantify the uncertainty, we need to know the value of covariance for all the possible pairs of measurements. This is practically not feasible. In this case, an upper bound can be placed on the uncertainty. If $X_1, X_2, …, X_n$ are $n$ dependent measurements and $c_1, c_2, …, c_n$ are constants, then the uncertainty of $c_1X_1 + c_2X_2 + … + c_nX_n$ can be bounded as:
